title: Add whoAmI() backend function and temporary frontend test button

requirements.text: Add a `whoAmI` public shared function to the backend actor in `backend/main.mo` that returns the caller's principal as Text. The function signature must be: `public shared({ caller }) func whoAmI() : async Text { return Principal.toText(caller); }`. No other existing logic may be modified.
acceptanceCriteria: The `whoAmI` function exists in `backend/main.mo` with exactly the specified signature
Calling `whoAmI()` returns the caller's principal as a Text string
No other existing functions or state in `backend/main.mo` are modified

requirements.text: Add a temporary debug button in the frontend that calls `actor.whoAmI()` and displays the result using `alert()` or `console.log()`. Place this button in a visible location (e.g., the admin dashboard page or login page) so it is easy to test. The button should be clearly labeled 'Test whoAmI'. No other existing frontend logic or UI may be changed.

quotes: Tambahkan sementara tombol di frontend yang memanggil actor.whoAmI() dan tampilkan hasilnya dengan alert atau console.log.

acceptanceCriteria: A button labeled 'Test whoAmI' is visible in the frontend UI
Clicking the button calls `actor.whoAmI()` using the existing actor from `useActor`
The result (caller principal as Text) is displayed via `alert()` or logged via `console.log()`
No other existing frontend components, hooks, routes, or logic are modified

constraints: Do not modify any existing logic in backend/main.mo beyond adding the whoAmI function
Do not modify any existing frontend components, hooks, routes, or pages beyond adding the temporary test button
The whoAmI function must use exactly the shared({ caller }) pattern specified by the user
nonGoals: Persisting or storing the whoAmI result anywhere
Adding any new routes or pages for this feature
Styling or polishing the temporary test button
Removing or cleaning up the temporary button (that is a future task)
